data.label: Choose a Language
data.description: Select a primary programming language. Your choice will influence which frameworks and tools you use. Node.js (JavaScript), Python, and Go are popular choices.
objectives: Understand basic syntax, data types, and control flow.
Learn how to handle package management for your chosen language (e.g., NPM, Pip).

data.label: Version Control Systems
data.description: Learn how to use Git, the most popular version control system, to track changes in your code and collaborate with others.
objectives: Understand Git basics: commit, push, pull, branch, merge.
Learn how to use a hosting service like GitHub or GitLab.

data.label: Databases
data.description: Learn how to store and retrieve data. Understand the differences between relational (SQL) and non-relational (NoSQL) databases.
objectives: Learn basic SQL queries (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).
Understand when to use SQL vs. NoSQL.
Connect a database to your application.

data.label: APIs & Communication
data.description: Learn how to create APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) to allow the frontend to communicate with your backend.
objectives: Learn how to build REST APIs.
Get an introduction to GraphQL as an alternative.

data.label: Authentication
data.description: Learn how to secure your application by verifying user identities.
objectives: Learn about OAuth for third-party logins.

data.label: Testing
data.description: Learn how to write tests for your backend to ensure it is reliable and bug-free.
objectives: Write unit tests for individual functions.
Write integration tests to check how components work together.
